meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Client Workflow to Finish AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your coding projects. Master this professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first AI app with confidence.
content.answer_block: To finish your projects, you must treat your personal builds like professional client engagements. By defining clear requirements, setting strict scope boundaries, and utilizing AI-assisted coding tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform nebulous ideas into shippable milestones, ensuring consistent progress and preventing burnout.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and endless feature creep. You start with high energy, but soon the architecture feels heavy, the bugs pile up, and the initial vision dissolves into a chaotic mess of half-baked code. It is not a lack of talent that halts your progress; it is a lack of structure.

Professional engineers don't rely on motivation; they rely on a rigorous client workflow. By treating your student project as if you were building for a high-paying client, you impose the constraints necessary to force completion. It is time to stop being a hobbyist and start being an AI builder.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Identify the one core feature that provides value. Document it as a rigid requirement to prevent feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Review my project idea and define the absolute minimum scope required for a functional MVP. List 3 'must-have' features and 5 'out-of-scope' features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project requirements.md and output a strict MVP scope list.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how your data flows. A solid schema prevents refactoring hell later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for my MVP. Include table names, key fields, and foreign key relationships. Output in Mermaid diagram format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a prisma schema file based on the defined MVP scope.'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Development
steps.description: Break the project into 4-hour sprints. Focus on completing one module at a time to maintain momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break my project roadmap into 4-hour development sprints. For each sprint, define the specific task and the success criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Create a tasks.md file with a 4-hour sprint breakdown.'

steps.title: Automate Testing and Validation
steps.description: Use AI to write tests for your core logic, ensuring that new code doesn't break existing progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the core logic module using Jest. Ensure all edge cases defined in the scope are covered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute unit tests for the authentication module.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Cleanup
steps.description: Prepare your codebase for production. Remove hardcoded credentials and optimize for performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Suggest a production-ready deployment checklist.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the codebase for environment variable leaks and suggest cleanup.'

pro_tips: Always write your README before writing code; it forces you to think about the user experience first.
If a feature takes more than two hours to implement, break it into two smaller, independent tasks.
Use 'Gitmoji' in your commit messages to visually track your progress through the project lifecycle.
Never start a new feature until the current one has a passing test suite.
Allocate 20% of your time to refactoring; technical debt is the primary cause of abandoned projects.
content.next_steps: After completing your first MVP, perform a post-mortem analysis of your workflow, document your challenges in a blog post, and begin planning your next project with a 20% increase in complexity.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap. The only thing left is to write the first line of code. Don't let your ideas stay in your head—bring them to life. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
